assertions.expectation: Addresses Istio control plane version compatibility with the target Kubernetes version
assertions.reason: Response includes detailed compatibility matrix showing GKE 1.32 requires Istio 1.20+ minimum and recommends Istio 1.22+ for full compatibility

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the Istio-Kubernetes version compatibility matrix before upgrading GKE
assertions.reason: Response explicitly includes a 'Pre-Flight: Version Compatibility Check' section with compatibility matrix and states 'If your Istio version is < 1.20, stop here and upgrade Istio first'

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading Istio first if the current Istio version doesn't support the target Kubernetes version
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'If your Istio version is < 1.20, stop here and upgrade Istio first before GKE' and explains this in the compatibility section

assertions.expectation: Explains that sidecar injection happens via mutating webhook and may cause issues during node drain/recreation
assertions.reason: Response mentions admission controller conflicts and includes checking mutating webhook configurations, plus discusses sidecar reconnection issues during node restarts

assertions.expectation: Mentions that new pods on upgraded nodes will get sidecar injected — verify the sidecar version matches
assertions.reason: Response includes Phase 4 'Data Plane (Sidecar) Upgrade' with specific command to verify sidecar versions across pods and discusses version consistency

assertions.expectation: Recommends monitoring Istio proxy version consistency across the mesh after upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Monitoring During Upgrade' section with commands to check Envoy proxy health and mentions monitoring proxy version consistency as a critical concern

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check Istio version and proxy sync status (istioctl or kubectl)
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple commands including 'kubectl get pods -n istio-system -l app=istiod', proxy health checks via 'kubectl exec', and service discovery sync checks

assertions.expectation: Warns about potential traffic disruption if Istio control plane and sidecar versions are mismatched
assertions.reason: Response warns about 'Envoy Version Skew' issues and explains that version mismatches can cause mesh partitioning and connectivity problems, with specific mitigation steps
